uplink.description: VOC (0x0C) legacy-sensor datalog frame: 2 measurements (date temperature humidity pressure lux voc, 2-byte VOC) + battery-percentage byte. Newest measurement at top level; the older one (35.50 %RH, 1234 lx, 850 VOC) in history. Inputs synthetic; expected values produced by running reference/upstream-codec.js parseVOC as the oracle.
uplink._note: TTN codec is the oracle. Input bytes are synthetic in-bounds values; expected.data was produced by decoding the same bytes through reference/upstream-codec.js (uplink id 0x0C parseVOC, isNew=false) and re-keying its flat output to the normalized vocabulary (temperature->air.temperature, humidity->air.relativeHumidity, pressure->air.pressure hPa, lux->air.lightIntensity lux, voc->tvoc, battery%->batteryPercent). Upstream flattens the two records to one object keeping the newest values; we keep the older record in history.

uplink.description: VOC (0x12) new-sensor datalog frame: 2 measurements with a 3-byte VOC field (date temperature humidity pressure lux voc) + battery-percentage byte. Same as 0x0C except VOC is 24-bit (newest VOC 66048). Inputs synthetic; expected values produced from reference/upstream-codec.js parseVOC (isNew=true) as the oracle.
uplink._note: TTN codec is the oracle. Input bytes synthetic; expected.data produced by decoding the same bytes through reference/upstream-codec.js (uplink id 0x12 parseVOC, isNew=true), which reads VOC as a 24-bit little-endian value (here bytes 0,2,1 -> 66048). Re-keyed to the normalized vocabulary as for the 0x0C vector.

uplink.description: TER (0x04) datalog frame, 3 measurements (no lux/VOC): temperature + humidity + pressure. Shares the MCF88 base record with the CO2/VOC variants. Inputs from the Enginko data-frame-format worked example; expected values produced via reference/upstream-codec.js parseTER as the oracle.
uplink._note: TTN codec is the oracle. Bytes taken from the Enginko data_frame_format TER worked example (also used by the sibling mcf-lw12co2). Expected.data produced by decoding through reference/upstream-codec.js (uplink id 0x04 parseTER) and re-keying to the normalized vocabulary; the trailing battery/rfu bytes are absent in this 31-byte example so no batteryPercent is emitted.

uplink.description: Unsupported frame id (0x09 metering) is rejected.
uplink._note: 0x09 (metering) is outside this codec's supported frame set; the codec rejects unknown ids before any record parsing.
